_description: Retrieval benchmark gabarito (régua), regenerated 2026-06-27 from the live 34004-chunk index (.data/rag_index/chunks.json) by deterministic name-anchor join (tests/rag/regenerate_qrels.py). 13 reverse-joined from the original alex-hormozi gabarito + 30 forward-generated across 10 other persons (heuristicas/frameworks). Each relevant_chunk_id is a REAL live chunk_id whose `name` matches the query anchor; answer_text is that chunk's text. NO fabricated mappings; unresolved/ambiguous anchors DROPPED + logged (Art. IV + extraction-no-fallbacks). See docs/stories/epic-RETRIEVAL-BENCHMARK/STORY-RBM-QRELS-REBUILD.md.

queries.query: What is the specific metric or threshold for: Only 3 real objections exist in any sales conversation?
queries.answer_text:
ID: INS-AH-003
name: Only 3 real objections exist in any sales conversation
description: Price (value discrepancy), Stall (need more time), Decision Maker (need to consult someone). Drill scripts for all three — nothing else matters.
quote: There's only three, by the way, if you don't know. They are: it's price, it's stall, it's decision maker.
source_chunks: AH-chunk-013-02
source_id: AH-0013
priority: HIGH
queries.label: alex-hormozi/heuristicas

queries.query: What is the specific metric or threshold for: 70-20-10 Content Rule?
queries.answer_text:
ID: INS-AH-027
name: 70-20-10 Content Rule
description: 70% proven formats, 20% variations of proven formats, 10% experimental. Apply at every scale — beginners often invert it.
quote: 70% of your content should be proven: stuff that works. 20% should be variations of proven stuff. 10% should be experiments. Most creators do it backwards.
source_chunks: AH-chunk-018-02
source_id: AH-0018
priority: HIGH
queries.label: alex-hormozi/heuristicas

queries.query: What is the specific metric or threshold for: 30-Second Story rule — illustrate each concept in 30 seconds?
queries.answer_text:
ID: INS-AH-044
name: 30-Second Story rule — illustrate each concept in 30 seconds
description: Each concept in a sales script should be illustrated by a 30-second story. Stories are memorable, relatable, and do not feel like a pitch. Long explanations kill momentum.
quote: You're going to give a short 30-second snippet of why that's important. 30-second story. They understand it. Long explanations kill deals.
source_chunks: AH-chunk-013-01
source_id: AH-0013
priority: HIGH
queries.label: alex-hormozi/heuristicas

queries.query: What is the specific metric or threshold for: Promote in first 30 days — give them something to call mom about?
queries.answer_text:
ID: INS-AH-073
name: Promote in first 30 days — give them something to call mom about
description: New hires should receive their first meaningful promotion or title upgrade within the first 30 days. Early wins create emotional investment and retention. People need something to be proud of quickly — a reason to call home.
quote: Promote them in the first 30 days. Give them something to call mom about. That emotional buy-in in the first month is what keeps them.
source_chunks: AH-chunk-020-04
source_id: AH-0020
priority: HIGH
queries.label: alex-hormozi/heuristicas

queries.query: What is the specific metric or threshold for: Weekly 1-on-1 structure — Personal then Short-term then Long-term?
queries.answer_text:
ID: INS-AH-071
name: Weekly 1-on-1 structure — Personal then Short-term then Long-term
description: Weekly 1-on-1 meetings with reps follow a 3-part structure. Start with personal check-in (how are you doing as a human), then short-term performance (this week's numbers and call reviews), then long-term development (career path and skill gaps). Order matters — personal first builds trust.
quote: Start personal. Then short-term — what happened this week. Then long-term — where are you going. Always in that order. Personal first builds the trust.
source_chunks: AH-chunk-020-03
source_id: AH-0020
priority: HIGH
queries.label: alex-hormozi/frameworks

queries.answer_text:
ID: INS-AH-014
name: 14-Day Onboarding Ramp for new sales reps
description: Days 1-3: product knowledge. Days 4-7: script mastery. Days 8-14: live calls with coaching. No solo calls until day 8 minimum.
quote: The ramp period for a new rep is 14 days. First week is learning the product and script until they can recite it. Second week is live calls with oversight.

queries.query: What is the specific metric or threshold for: 2-week onramp with 3 milestones (script then recaps then close/objection)?
queries.answer_text:
ID: INS-AH-077
name: 2-week onramp with 3 milestones (script then recaps then close/objection)
description: New sales rep onboarding runs exactly 2 weeks with 3 clear milestones. Week 1 milestone — pass script recitation test. Week 1.5 milestone — demonstrate proper call recaps. Week 2 milestone — pass close and objection handling drill. Each milestone is binary pass/fail with no partial credit.
quote: Two weeks. Three milestones. First, recite the script cold. Second, show me proper recaps. Third, close me and handle my objections. Pass or fail. No gray area.
source_chunks: AH-chunk-020-04, AH-chunk-020-05
source_id: AH-0020
priority: HIGH
queries.label: alex-hormozi/metodologias

queries.query: What is the specific metric or threshold for: 3 question types — yes-based, neutral, no-based?
queries.answer_text:
ID: INS-AH-007
name: 3 question types — yes-based, neutral, no-based
description: Three question types for sales conversations. No-based questions are most powerful because they eliminate decision fatigue and feel safe to answer.
quote: A yes-based question would be 'Sound great?' A neutral type would be 'Fair enough?' And the no-based: 'Would you be opposed to moving forward today?'

source_id: AH-0015
